title: Enhancing Project Dashboards with AI PC Automation

posts.author: harrycmary (Harry)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi everyone,

I’m exploring ways to make project dashboards smarter and more dynamic. Currently, adding new boards and updating dashboards manually takes a lot of time and can lead to errors in reporting. I started experimenting with AI PC to automate parts of this process, and I’d love to hear how others are approaching it.

Here’s what I’m trying so far:

  • Using AI PC to auto-populate dashboards when new project boards are created.

  • Generating real-time progress and capacity summaries across multiple boards.

  • Highlighting bottlenecks or overdue tasks automatically using AI PC insights.

I’m curious about your experiences:

  1. How do you integrate AI PC to make dashboards more dynamic?

  2. Which visualizations or metrics have been most useful for your projects?

  3. Are there any templates or workflow hacks you’ve found helpful?

  4. Have you run into limitations or challenges while automating dashboards?

It would be great to share examples or screenshots of dashboards enhanced with AI PC. Any tips, tricks, or lessons learned would be very helpful!

Thanks in advance for sharing your insights.

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello @harrycmary Great question. Many teams run into this as dashboards scale.

AI PC works best as an insight layer, not the structure itself. Most setups use board and dashboard templates with connected boards so new project boards are added automatically, then AI PC summarizes progress, risks, and overdue work.

What works well:
Standardized board templates with consistent columns.
Dashboard templates that auto include new boards.
Status, timeline, workload, and numbers widgets paired with AI summaries.

Key limits:
AI PC doesn’t replace templates or connections.
Clean, consistent data is critical.
AI insights support decisions, not automations.

Dr. Tanvi Sachar
Monday Certified Partner, Tuesday Wizard
